mechanism_narrative: A falling or near-zero FEDFUNDS path lowers the discount rate on equities and signals policy support for risk assets, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the rate is pinned close to zero.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not higher than unconditional returns when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 9%, labor-market stress signals falling household income and weaker earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if mean triggered returns are not below baseline at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Positive RSAFS momentum combined with non-declining PAYEMS indicates that consumer demand is recovering without renewed job losses, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not outperform cases where only RSAFS improves by at least 1 percentage point annualized over 60 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: A HOUST reading below 600 thousand units signals severe housing and construction weakness that depresses cyclical earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if observations with HOUST below 600 have nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or do not underperform observations with HOUST above 600.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in INDPRO signals near-term improvement in production volumes and operating leverage, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO monthly changes are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of cases or if the sign hit rate is below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL inflation combined with a rising FEDFUNDS path signals tightening pressure and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation crosses above 4% while FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-and-rate trigger has positive mean 20-day S&P 500 returns or if it does not underperform periods with high CPIAUCSL inflation alone.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth crosses above zero after weakness, aggregate demand has exited contraction and expected earnings recover, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth crossings above zero are not followed by positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if triggered returns fail to exceed baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp PAYEMS decline combined with a rising UNRATE indicates accelerating job-market deterioration and earnings risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change crosses below -250 thousand while UNRATE rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor deterioration trigger is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations or has nonnegative mean return.
